name: Honey Walnut Shrimp
directions: Melt butter in sauce pan on high heat, add walnuts and sugar then lower heat to medium for 1-2 minutes then remove from heat.
Combine all ingredients for sauce and mix until smooth put a side.
Heat: 2-3 cups of oil on high heat.

Mix flour, sugar, salt and water in a bowl until smooth. Rinse raw shrimp and add to mix one by one dropping shrimp into the hot oil for 2 minutes be careful not to overcrowd the pot. Once the shrimp turns LIGHT BROWN place the cooked shrimp on a dish layered with paper towel to absorb the oil. Once all shrimp is cooked put in a dish and mix with your cooked walnuts and spread the sauce over the shrimp and gently mix.

name: Sushi – Bar Almonds
directions: Preheat the oven to 250°F Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Mix the sugar, salt, ginger and wasabi powder in a small bowl.
Place the almonds in a large bowl.
Whisk together the tapioca starch and water and pour over the almonds and toss to coat.
Add the spice mixture and toss until well coated.
Spread the nuts out in one layer on the baking sheet; bake until dry, about 45 minutes. Let cool completely on the baking sheet.

name: Honey Walnut Shrimps
directions: Stir together the water and honey in a quart size pot.  Bring to a boil and add the walnuts.  Boil for 2 minutes, then drain and place walnuts on a cookie sheet to dry overnight.  Heat oil in a wok and deep fry the walnuts and remove and drain into a baking pan line with paper towel and a wire rack.  Pour the oil into a can and cool and place into the garbage can.

Place the cornstarch and salt into a bowl and add some of the shrimps and coat the shrimp and remove extras cornstarch and deep fry in oil for a minute or two and drain on a rack.  Repeat is step until done.  Cool the oil and pour into a glass jar for use favor oil for stir fry.
Assemble sauce by whisking together ingredients until smooth.  Make 4 cups of sauce.  Place the extra sauce onto a plastic container and cover and into refrigerator.
In a mixing bowl, combine hot cooked shrimp and one cup of the walnut shrimp’s sauce until thoroughly coated.  To garnish, top with honey walnuts sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds.

name: Cashews Thai Style
directions: Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat, add in the shredded lime leaves and chilli flakes and fry just until the leaves change colour.
Remove from the heat and drain off any oil you can. Add the cashews to the pan and stir over a low heat for a minute or so.
Remove from the heat and add the shallots, garlic and salt to taste. Try to not eat the lot!

name: Whole Wheat Sesame Noodles
directions: microwave ingredients including 2 T of water. stir well. add hot water until consistency of heavy cream.
when noodles are done toss in a handful of cherry tomatoes, drain, rinse with cold water.
stir sauce into noodles top with diced green onions and sesame seeds.

name: Shoyu Wasabi Roasted Almonds
directions: Line a baking sheet with non-stick aluminum foil or parchment paper. Pre-heat oven to 350.
Spread almonds on the sheet to a single layer. Bake for 10 minutes.
While the nuts are baking, combine marinade ingredients in a stainless mixing bowl large enough to accomodate all the nuts and whisk to combine.

After the almonds have baked for the 10 minutes pour them into the marinade and stir to evenly coat them and then put them back on the sheet in a single layer and bake for an additonal 10 to 15 minutes stirring the nuts at about 7 minutes or so so that they will bake evenly. Taste one at ten minutes and see if is roasted to your liking if not continue until they are done.

Rinse and dry your mixing bowl and combine the dry ingredients in it. When the nuts are done roasting, toss them while they are still hot in the dry ingredients (I usually use a food service disposable glove and press the powder into the nuts). Drop the nuts into a wired strainer over a plate and shake the excess powder off. Warning! Done this way the nuts are a wasabi bomb so if you like it milder, use less powder and a lighter coating.

name: Vietnamese-Style Cod, Prawn & Coconut Curry
directions: Put the oil in a large pan over a high heat. Add the garlic, ginger and shallot and fry for 30secs-1 min until just starting to turn golden brown. Add the curry powder, turmeric and chilli, and stir-fry for 30 secs.
Add the tomato puree, coconut milk, stock, sugar, lemongrass, bay leaves, and fish sauce. Bring to the boil, then simmer until thickened.

Add the cod and prawns, and bring back to the boil. Cover and simmer for 5 mins, until the cod is cooked through and the prawns are pink. Stir in the lime juice, garnish with coriander and chillies, if using, and serve with rice.
